1→# WC3 Build Order Helper — Architecture & Design
     2→
     3→## Context
     4→Learning project to build a Warcraft 3 build order practice tool using React/Redux, Node.js, PostgreSQL, and REST APIs. The app runs on a side monitor while playing WC3, providing a timer that plays sound cues at build order timestamps.
     5→
     6→---
     7→
     8→## Tech Stack
     9→
    10→| Layer | Technology |
    11→|-------|-----------|
    12→| Frontend | React 18 (Vite + JSX), Redux Toolkit, React Router v6 |
    13→| Backend | Node.js + Express |
    14→| Database | PostgreSQL + `pg` (node-postgres) |
    15→| Audio | Web Audio API (precise scheduling) |
    16→| Styling | CSS Modules (simple, no extra deps) |
    17→
    18→**Why Vite over CRA:** Faster dev server, simpler config, CRA is deprecated.
    19→**Why Redux Toolkit over plain Redux:** Still Redux under the hood, but less boilerplate — you learn the same concepts (store, slices, reducers, selectors, thunks).
    20→
    21→---
    22→
    23→## Project Structure
    24→
    25→```
    26→wc3-react/
    27→├── client/                  # React frontend
    28→│   ├── public/
    29→│   │   └── sounds/          # Audio files (.mp3/.wav)
    30→│   ├── src/
    31→│   │   ├── main.jsx         # Entry point
    32→│   │   ├── App.jsx          # Routes + layout
    33→│   │   ├── store/           # Redux store
    34→│   │   │   ├── index.js     # configureStore
    35→│   │   │   ├── buildOrdersSlice.js
    36→│   │   │   ├── timerSlice.js
    37→│   │   │   └── racesSlice.js
    38→│   │   ├── pages/
    39→│   │   │   ├── Home.jsx            # Race selection
    40→│   │   │   ├── BuildOrderList.jsx  # Browse/pick build orders for a race
    41→│   │   │   ├── BuildOrderEditor.jsx # Create/edit a build order
    42→│   │   │   └── Player.jsx          # THE MAIN SCREEN: timer + steps + sounds
    43→│   │   ├── components/
    44→│   │   │   ├── Timer.jsx           # Countdown/countup display + controls

    74→```
    75→
    76→---
    77→
    78→## Database Schema
    79→
    80→```sql
    81→CREATE TABLE races (
    82→  id    SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    83→  name  VARCHAR(20) UNIQUE NOT NULL,  -- 'Human', 'Orc', 'Undead', 'Night Elf'
    84→  icon  VARCHAR(255)                   -- path or URL to race icon
    85→);
    86→
    87→CREATE TABLE build_orders (
    88→  id          SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    89→  race_id     INTEGER REFERENCES races(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    90→  name        VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,       -- e.g. "HU Fast Expand"
    91→  description TEXT,
    92→  matchup     VARCHAR(10),                 -- e.g. "HUvOC", "ANY"
    93→  is_premade  BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
    94→  created_at  TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
    95→);
    96→
    97→CREATE TABLE build_order_steps (
    98→  id              SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
    99→  build_order_id  INTEGER REFERENCES build_orders(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
   100→  timestamp_secs  INTEGER NOT NULL,         -- seconds from game start (170 = 2:50)
   101→  action          VARCHAR(200) NOT NULL,    -- "Upgrade to Castle"
   102→  category        VARCHAR(30),              -- 'build', 'upgrade', 'unit', 'hero', 'timing'
   103→  sound_cue       VARCHAR(100),             -- filename in /sounds/ (null = use TTS or default beep)
   104→  sort_order      INTEGER NOT NULL
   105→);
   106→```
   107→
   108→**Key decisions:**
   109→- `timestamp_secs` is an integer (seconds), not a time type — simpler to compare against the timer
   110→- `sort_order` separate from `timestamp_secs` because multiple actions can happen at the same timestamp
   111→- `sound_cue` is optional — when null, use a default notification sound or browser Speech Synthesis API for text-to-speech
   112→
   113→---
   114→
   115→## API Endpoints
   116→
   117→| Method | Endpoint | Description |
   118→|--------|----------|-------------|
   119→| GET | `/api/races` | List all 4 races |
   120→| GET | `/api/build-orders?raceId=1` | List build orders, filter by race |
   121→| GET | `/api/build-orders/:id` | Get single build order with all steps |
   122→| POST | `/api/build-orders` | Create build order + steps |
   123→| PUT | `/api/build-orders/:id` | Update build order + steps |
   124→| DELETE | `/api/build-orders/:id` | Delete build order |
   125→
   126→Request/response bodies use JSON. Steps are nested inside build order responses:
   127→```json
   128→{
   129→  "id": 1,
   130→  "name": "HU Fast Expand",
   131→  "race": "Human",
   132→  "steps": [
   133→    { "timestamp_secs": 0, "action": "Train Peasant", "category": "unit" },
   134→    { "timestamp_secs": 15, "action": "Send Peasant to gold", "category": "timing" },
   135→    { "timestamp_secs": 170, "action": "Upgrade to Keep", "category": "upgrade" }
   136→  ]
   137→}
   138→```
   139→
   140→---
   141→
   142→## Redux Store Shape
   143→
   144→```js
   145→{
   146→  races: {
   147→    items: [],          // [{ id, name, icon }]
   148→    status: 'idle'      // 'idle' | 'loading' | 'succeeded' | 'failed'
   149→  },
   150→  buildOrders: {
   151→    items: [],          // list view
   152→    current: null,      // single build order with steps (for Player/Editor)
   153→    status: 'idle'
   154→  },
   155→  timer: {
   156→    elapsedSecs: 0,     // current time in seconds
   157→    isRunning: false,
   158→    speed: 1,           // playback speed multiplier (1x, 1.5x, 2x)
   159→    completedStepIds: [] // steps already triggered
   160→  }
   161→}
   162→```
   163→
   164→---
   165→
   166→## Timer & Audio Mechanism
   167→
   168→This is the core feature. Two separate concerns:
   169→
   170→### Timer (useTimer hook)
   171→- Uses `requestAnimationFrame` for smooth UI updates
   172→- Tracks elapsed time with `performance.now()` (not setInterval — avoids drift)
   173→- Dispatches `timerTick` to Redux every ~100ms with current `elapsedSecs`
   174→- Supports: start, pause, resume, reset, speed adjustment
   175→
   176→### Audio Scheduling (services/audio.js)
   177→- On **play**, pre-load all sound files for the current build order
   178→- Each tick, check if any step's `timestamp_secs <= elapsedSecs` and hasn't been triggered yet
   179→- When a step triggers: play its sound cue + visually highlight it in StepList
   180→- For steps without a custom sound file, use `SpeechSynthesisUtterance` to read the action text aloud (browser TTS — zero audio files needed for basic functionality)
   181→- Web Audio API `AudioContext` for low-latency playback
   182→
   183→### Why not pre-schedule with Web Audio API setTimeout?
   184→Pause/resume/speed-change make pre-scheduling complex. Simpler to check on each tick.
   185→
   186→---
   187→
   188→## Frontend Pages & Flow
   189→
   190→```
   191→Home (/) → Pick a race
   192→  ↓
   193→BuildOrderList (/race/:raceId) → Browse/search build orders for that race
   194→  ↓
   195→Player (/play/:buildOrderId) → THE MAIN SCREEN
   196→  - Large timer display at top
   197→  - Step list below (current step highlighted, upcoming visible, past greyed)
   198→  - Controls: Play/Pause, Reset, Speed (1x/1.5x/2x)
   199→  - Auto-scrolls to current step
   200→
   201→BuildOrderEditor (/build-orders/new, /build-orders/:id/edit)
   202→  - Form to name the build order, pick race
   203→  - Add steps: timestamp (mm:ss picker) + action text + category dropdown
   204→  - Reorder steps via drag or sort buttons
   205→```
   206→
   207→### Player Page Layout (side monitor optimized)
   208→```
   209→┌─────────────────────┐
   210→│      02:50           │  ← large timer
   211→│  ▶ Pause  ↺ Reset   │  ← controls
   212→├─────────────────────┤
   213→│ ✓ 0:00  Train Peon  │  ← completed (grey)
   214→│ ✓ 0:15  Altar       │
   215→│ ► 2:50  Keep ←──────│  ← CURRENT (highlighted + sound)
   216→│   3:30  Blacksmith   │  ← upcoming
   217→│   4:00  Tech         │
   218→└─────────────────────┘
   219→```
   220→
   221→Compact, vertical, readable at a glance from your gaming monitor.
   222→
   223→---
   224→
   225→## Implementation Order
   226→
   227→### Phase 1: Project Scaffolding
   228→1. Initialize root `package.json` with `concurrently` for running client + server
   229→2. Scaffold client with `npm create vite@latest client -- --template react`
   230→3. Set up server with Express, basic health check endpoint

   232→5. Seed the 4 races
   233→
   234→### Phase 2: Backend API
   235→6. Implement `GET /api/races`
   236→7. Implement CRUD for `/api/build-orders` (with nested steps)
   237→8. Seed 2-3 premade build orders (e.g., HU Fast Expand, OC Blademaster Rush)
   238→9. Test endpoints with curl or Postman
   239→
   240→### Phase 3: Frontend Foundation
   241→10. Install Redux Toolkit, React Router, set up store
   242→11. Build Home page (race selection)
   243→12. Build BuildOrderList page (fetch + display build orders)
   244→13. Wire up API calls via `services/api.js`
   245→
   246→### Phase 4: The Player (Core Feature)
   247→14. Build `useTimer` hook with `requestAnimationFrame`
   248→15. Build Timer component (display + controls)
   249→16. Build StepList/StepItem with active step highlighting + auto-scroll
   250→17. Implement audio service (TTS for step actions)
   251→18. Connect timer ticks to step triggering + sound playback
   252→
   253→### Phase 5: Build Order Editor
   254→19. Build the form for creating/editing build orders
   255→20. Add step management (add, remove, reorder steps)
   256→21. Wire up POST/PUT API calls
   257→
   258→### Phase 6: Polish
   259→22. Add speed controls (1x, 1.5x, 2x)
   260→23. Add custom sound files for common actions
   261→24. Responsive/compact styling for side monitor
   262→25. Error handling and loading states
   263→
   264→---
   265→
   266→## Verification / How to Test
   267→1. `npm run dev` from root starts both client (Vite on :5173) and server (Express on :3000)
   268→2. Visit http://localhost:5173, select a race, pick a premade build order
   269→3. Hit play — timer counts up, steps highlight as timestamps are reached, sounds/TTS play
   270→4. Create a custom build order via the editor, verify it saves and appears in the list
   271→5. Test pause/resume/reset/speed controls during playback

);
